topic: Stop building AI tools. Start building AI processes.
hook_summary: Everyone's building AI tools. That's why most fail.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone's building AI tools right now. That's why most will fail in 90 days.
scenes.visual_prompt: A 28-year-old man with short dark wavy hair and light stubble, wearing a dark polo shirt, sitting at a corner table in a busy coffee shop. Natural overhead lighting, candid documentary style. Medium close-up, locked-off shot. He looks directly into the camera, slightly leaning forward.

scenes.audio_text: I spent six months and forty thousand dollars building a custom AI agent for cold outreach. It never worked.
scenes.visual_prompt: Same man, now standing in a small, cluttered office near a whiteboard covered in scribbles. Harsh fluorescent light, but warm tone. Over-shoulder shot showing the whiteboard and his profile as he gestures toward the board. Slow push-in.

scenes.audio_text: Then I stripped it down. One simple automation routing inbound leads straight to my calendar. No fancy interface, no chatbot. Just a Zapier -> Calendly flow. That one process replaced the entire tool.
scenes.visual_prompt: Same man, walking down a quiet city street during golden hour. Soft side light, documentary feel. Wide shot as he walks, then cut to medium close-up as he stops and faces the camera. Natural motion.

scenes.audio_text: Stop building AI tools. Start building AI processes. That's the whole thing.
scenes.visual_prompt: Same man, now sitting on a park bench with trees in background, dappled sunlight. Medium close-up, locked-off. He gives a slight nod after speaking. Photo-real, natural light.
